title: Manti Turkish Dumplings Yogurt

seo.title: Manti Recipe: Authentic Turkish Dumplings with Yogurt
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Turkish Manti dumplings with this easy-to-follow recipe. Delicious homemade pasta served with garlicky yogurt and spiced butter. Perfect for dinner!
seo.h1: Authentic Manti Recipe: Turkish Dumplings with Yogurt Sauce
h2Headings: How to Make Manti: Step-by-Step Guide
seo.imageAlt: Plate of authentic Turkish Manti dumplings with garlic yogurt sauce and spiced butter, garnished with mint.
seo.imageCaption: A close-up of delicious homemade Turkish Manti, ready to be enjoyed with a generous dollop of creamy garlic yogurt.

description: Manti are exquisite Turkish dumplings, tiny parcels of dough filled with a savory spiced minced meat mixture. These delicate pasta pockets are boiled to perfection and then generously bathed in a creamy, garlicky yogurt sauce, finished with a sizzling drizzle of vibrant red pepper-infused butter. It's a true comfort food masterpiece, offering a delightful blend of textures and bold flavors that transport you straight to the heart of Turkish cuisine.

ingredients.notes: plus more as needed for the dough

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 3 cups all-purpose flour and 1 teaspoon salt. Make a well in the center, add 1 large egg and gradually incorporate 1/2 cup water, adding more if needed, until a firm, shaggy dough forms. Transfer to a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic. Cover the dough with a clean cloth or plastic wrap and let it rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Make the Meat Filling
instructions.text: While the dough rests, prepare the savory meat filling. In a medium bowl, combine 250g ground beef or lamb, 1 small finely grated onion, 2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, and 1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes (if using). Mix all ingredients thoroughly with your hands until well combined and uniform.

instructions.title: Roll and Cut the Dough
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into two equal portions. On a lightly floured surface, take one portion and roll it out very thinly, about 1-2 mm thick, into a large square or rectangle. Using a sharp knife or a pastry wheel, carefully cut the dough into very small squares, approximately 1.5 cm by 1.5 cm. Repeat with the second dough portion.

instructions.title: Fill and Shape the Manti
instructions.text: Place a tiny amount (about 1/4 teaspoon) of the meat filling in the center of each small dough square. To shape the manti, bring two opposite corners of the square together and pinch them firmly to seal. Then, bring the other two opposite corners together and pinch them firmly, creating a small, boat-like or purse-like shape. Ensure all edges are tightly sealed to prevent the filling from escaping during cooking. Repeat this process for all remaining dough squares and filling.

instructions.title: Cook the Manti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a rolling boil. Carefully add the shaped manti in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Cook for 8-10 minutes, or until the manti float to the surface and the dough is tender. Remove the cooked manti from the pot using a slotted spoon and transfer them directly to individual serving plates or a large platter.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauces
instructions.text: While the manti cooks, prepare the two essential sauces. For the garlic yogurt sauce, whisk together 2 cups plain full-fat yogurt, 2 minced garlic cloves, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a small bowl until smooth. For the spiced butter sauce, melt 4 tablespoons unsalted butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add 1 teaspoon red pepper flakes and 1 teaspoon dried mint (if using), and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant and the butter is lightly browned. Be careful not to burn the butter.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Arrange the hot, cooked manti on individual serving plates. Spoon a generous amount of the creamy garlic yogurt sauce over the manti, ensuring each dumpling is coated. Finish by drizzling the hot, spiced butter over the yogurt. Garnish with additional fresh mint leaves or a sprinkle of sumac if desired for extra flavor and color. Serve immediately and enjoy!

tips: For truly authentic Manti, try to make your dumplings as small as possible. The smaller they are, the more skilled and traditional they are considered.
If you don't have fresh ground meat, you can use pre-ground, but ensure it's not too lean as the fat adds flavor and moisture to the filling.
Manti is best served immediately. If you have leftovers, store the manti and sauces separately and combine just before reheating to maintain texture.
article: Manti Turkish Dumplings Yogurt is a beloved Turkish Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
